title: Koyfin Pricing Explained: All Plans, Costs & Fees (2026)
meta_title: Koyfin Pricing Explained: All Plans, Costs & Fees (2026) | TradingToolsHub
meta_description: Compare Koyfin pricing, free-plan limits and paid features to choose the right subscription for your investing workflow.
excerpt: Complete breakdown of Koyfin pricing — all plans, hidden fees, and how to save money.
content:
Koyfin Pricing Overview

Koyfin is a Bloomberg-alternative financial data platform designed specifically for retail investors who need institutional-grade tools without institutional-grade pricing. Founded in 2016 and headquartered in New York, Koyfin has built a reputation for delivering Bloomberg-quality data at roughly 1/50th of Bloomberg's cost. The platform starts at $0 per month for a free tier and scales up to $59 per month for the Enterprise plan, making it accessible to individual traders, portfolio managers, and fundamental analysts across different budget levels.

Unlike many fintech platforms that hide pricing behind contact forms or demo requests, Koyfin publishes clear, straightforward pricing upfront. This transparency is refreshing in an industry historically dominated by opaque enterprise sales models. The question for most traders isn't whether Koyfin is affordable—it clearly is—but rather which tier aligns with their actual research needs and whether the platform's strengths justify the subscription cost.

All Koyfin Plans Compared

Koyfin offers four distinct pricing tiers, each designed for different levels of market research activity and data requirements:

Feature Free Plus Pro Enterprise
Monthly Price $0 $27.50 $45 $59
Advanced Charting Basic ✓ Advanced ✓ Advanced ✓ Advanced
Real-Time Data Limited ✓ Full ✓ Full ✓ Full
Custom Indicators ✗ ✓ ✓ ✓
API Access ✗ ✗ ✓ ✓
Excel Integration ✗ ✗ ✓ ✓
Performance Analytics Basic ✓ Enhanced ✓ Enhanced ✓ Enhanced
Alerts & Screening Limited ✓ Full ✓ Full ✓ Full

Free Plan: The free tier includes basic charting, delayed data, limited real-time capabilities, and fundamental analysis tools. You cannot access custom indicators or API functionality, but you get a genuine taste of the platform's core research capabilities.

Plus ($27.50/month): This mid-tier plan activates real-time data, custom indicators, and enhanced screening capabilities. The Plus tier is ideal for active retail traders who need live market data but don't require programmatic access or Excel integration.

Pro ($45/month): The Pro tier introduces API access and Excel integration, essential for traders who build spreadsheet models, backtest strategies, or integrate Koyfin data into other platforms. This is where Koyfin becomes genuinely dangerous for portfolio managers and quant-minded investors.

Enterprise ($59/month): The top tier is positioned for professional users and small teams. It includes everything from the Pro plan plus priority support and additional customization options. For institutional budgets, $59 per month is essentially a rounding error compared to Bloomberg's ~$22,000+ annual cost.

Free Plan / Free Trial

Koyfin offers a genuine free tier rather than a limited trial period. This is a major advantage: you can use Koyfin indefinitely at no cost, though with limitations. The free plan lets you:

  • Access fundamental analysis and earnings data
  • Use basic charting and technical analysis
  • Screen stocks with fundamental filters
  • View delayed market data (typically 15-20 minute delays)
  • Research company financials and metrics
  • Set up basic alerts

What you cannot do on the free tier: access real-time data, build custom indicators, use the API, or export to Excel. For long-term investors conducting fundamental research outside of market hours, the free plan is genuinely sufficient. You won't get live data during market hours, but you will get comprehensive financial metrics, news feeds, and analysis tools that would cost thousands per month elsewhere.

Is it worth starting with the free plan? Absolutely. It's the risk-free way to evaluate whether Koyfin's data quality and interface match your research style. Many traders find the free tier covers 80% of their needs.

Hidden Costs and Fees

Koyfin's pricing is notably transparent, but several potential hidden costs deserve attention:

  • No trade execution: Koyfin is research and analysis only. You'll still need a separate brokerage to execute trades, which may carry their own commissions and fees. This isn't a hidden Koyfin cost, but it's part of the total cost of trading.
  • Data feed fees: Koyfin charges exchange fees for real-time data on Plus and above. These are included in the subscription and not itemized separately, but they're there.
  • No annual discount structure: Koyfin does not advertise annual billing discounts in publicly available pricing information. You pay monthly, making annual planning straightforward but potentially more expensive than platforms offering 20-30% annual discounts.
  • API rate limits: The Pro and Enterprise API access likely includes rate limits. Heavy automation may require upgrades not reflected in the published pricing.

Overall, the hidden cost risk with Koyfin is low. The platform avoids the nickel-and-diming approach common in fintech.

Koyfin Pricing vs Competitors

How does Koyfin stack up against comparable platforms? Here's an approximate comparison:

Platform Base Price Best For Key Advantage
Koyfin Free / $27.50–$59/mo Fundamental analysts Lowest cost; Bloomberg alternative
Finviz Free / ~$40/mo Stock screeners Excellent stock screening filters
TradingView Free / ~$15–$200/mo Technical analysis Superior charting; huge community
Bloomberg Terminal ~$22,000–$25,000/year Professional institutions Gold standard data; 30-year incumbency
FactSet ~$15,000+/year Institutional research Comprehensive data; analyst tools

Value Winner for Retail Traders: Koyfin dominates the price-to-value ratio for retail investors. At $59/month maximum, it costs less than one month of Bloomberg while delivering 70-80% of the functionality for equity research. TradingView is cheaper if you only need charting, but Koyfin beats it for fundamental analysis. Compared to Bloomberg and FactSet, Koyfin isn't even in the same galaxy—it's 1/50th the price.

Is Koyfin Worth the Price?

For fundamental analysts: Yes, absolutely. Koyfin's real-time earnings data, financial metrics, and comprehensive company research justify the Pro or Enterprise tier. If you conduct even basic fundamental analysis, Koyfin is worth $45/month.

For day traders and technicians: Koyfin is less essential. TradingView offers superior charting at lower cost. Koyfin shines for overnight research and fundamental screening, not intraday setups.

For portfolio managers: The Pro tier ($45/month) becomes critical when managing multiple positions. The Excel integration and API access enable portfolio tracking, correlation analysis, and performance attribution that would take hours to compile manually. This plan pays for itself in time savings.

For passive buy-and-hold investors: The free tier is likely sufficient. If you rebalance quarterly and hold index funds, you don't need real-time alerts or advanced screening. The free plan's financial metrics and news feed cover your due diligence.

Upgrading makes sense when: You actively screen stocks more than once per week, you build financial models in Excel, you backtest strategies, or you manage a portfolio of multiple positions. These use cases justify $45-59/month immediately.

How to Save on Koyfin

Koyfin's pricing is already lean, and the company doesn't advertise promotional discounts or student pricing tiers. However, cost optimization strategies exist:

  • Start free and upgrade gradually: Use the free tier for 30 days. Most traders find the free plan covers basic needs, eliminating the need to upgrade immediately.
  • Share a team plan: Enterprise pricing ($59/month) covers team usage. If multiple people in your organization need access, split the cost across team members.
  • Use annual savings on workflow: The API access on Pro tier can automate research workflows, saving time that translates to real money savings across your trading practice.
  • Leverage Excel integration: The Pro plan's Excel export eliminates expensive data subscription services for many traders. The integration alone can justify the upgrade if you build multi-ticker financial models.

Unlike competitors offering aggressive annual discounts, Koyfin maintains consistent pricing without promotional gimmicks. This stability is a feature, not a bug—it means long-term pricing certainty for your budget planning.

For a comprehensive comparison of Koyfin's strengths and weaknesses versus competing platforms, see our full Koyfin review. For broader evaluation of how Koyfin stacks up in the news and data feeds category, visit our news-data-feeds comparison page.
